Re
Fern Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CoulussroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
October 10,2003
Cynthia Curry
Department of General Services
Office of Legal Services
707 Tlird Street, 7ft Floor
West Sacramento, CA 95605
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-186
Dear Ms. Curry:
This letter is to notifu you that we have withdrawn the above captioned request
for advice relating to the undisclosed Department of General Services ("DGS") officet
and the conflict-of-interest issues of the Political Reform Act.l As of this date, you have
been unable to supply the information requested in the voicemail message I left for you
on September 9,2003, which was reiterated in my September 18,2003, correspondence.
This information is necessary to complete our analysis.
It is our policy, as stated in my September 18 correspondence, not to answer
purely hlpothetical questions, or questions where the facts presented are insufficient or
too vague to render specific advice. (Regulation ls3z9(b)e)@), (sxc) and (c)(a)@),
(F).) Further, it is our policy not to provide written advice for unnamed public officials.
(Regulation 18329(c)@)(B).) If you are able to obtain the requested information in the
future, please submit another request for written advice.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division
G',Wjg

18997 of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked about conflict-of-interest issues under the Political Reform Act relating to an undisclosed Department of General Services office, but the question could not be fully analyzed due to insufficient factual information.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because the requestor failed to provide necessary information requested on September 9 and reiterated on September 18, 2003. The FPPC does not answer purely hypothetical questions, questions with insufficient or vague facts, or provide written advice for unnamed public officials.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter, not a standard advice letter. No traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The document states the request was withdrawn due to insufficient information provided by the requestor and FPPC policy against advising on hypothetical or unnamed official scenarios.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor asked about conflict-of-interest issues under the Political Reform Act relating to an undisclosed Department of General Services office, but the question could not be fully analyzed due to insufficient factual information.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because the requestor failed to provide necessary information requested on September 9 and reiterated on September 18, 2003. The FPPC does not answer purely hypothetical questions, questions with insufficient or vague facts, or provide written advice for unnamed public officials.

embedding.summary: FPPC withdrawal letter for File No. W-03-186 regarding a DGS conflict-of-interest question that was withdrawn due to the requestor's failure to supply requested information needed to complete the analysis.
